title: Leykun Getaneh Gebeye
text:
Data Scientist · Statistician · Data Analyst

I am a Data Scientist, Statistician, and Data Analyst with over a decade of experience at the intersection of academic research and applied analytics. My work bridges statistics, machine learning, and deep learning — with a particular focus on applying data science to disease modeling, forecasting, and public health decision support systems.
I transform complex data into actionable insights using R, Python, SAS, Stata, and SPSS. My modeling toolkit spans Tidymodels, TensorFlow, and PyTorch, including transformer-based approaches like Temporal Fusion Transformers (TFT).
Beyond modeling, I build interactive data products and reproducible analytical workflows with R Shiny, Python Shiny, Streamlit, Plotly, and Quarto — communicating results through dynamic visualizations, dashboards, and automated reporting.
Research interests

Machine Learning and Deep Learning
Time Series Analysis and Forecasting
Disease Prediction and Modelling

title: Resume
text:
🧠 Summary
Data Scientist, Statistician, and Data Analyst with 10+ years of experience in academic and public health research. Skilled in R, Python, and statistical modeling with strong expertise in machine learning and emerging deep learning approaches (including transformer-based models such as TFT).
Experienced in building predictive models, interactive dashboards, and scalable analytics pipelines using tools like Shiny, Streamlit, and Quarto.
Focused on applying data science to disease modeling, forecasting, and public health decision support systems.

title: Projects
text: Projects I have built — spanning public health dashboards, predictive tools, and geospatial analytics.

title: Projects
text:
Malaria Forecasting Dashboard
An interactive malaria forecasting dashboard for public health monitoring and decision support. Built with R Shiny / Python Shiny, it provides real-time visualization of forecasted incidence trends and supports woreda-level, regional, and national public health decision-making.
→ View the application

title: Projects
text:
Ethiopia Earthquake Tracker
A real-time web application monitoring seismic activity across Ethiopia, built with R Shiny. With Ethiopia experiencing a series of earthquakes, this tool has become more essential than ever.
Key features: Live earthquake tracking via USGS · Interactive map visualization · Auto-refresh every 5 minutes · Magnitude range filtering
→ View the application

title: Projects
text:
Childhood Micronutrient Deficiency Prediction
An interactive prediction tool built with R Shiny that predicts childhood micronutrient deficiency using machine learning. The app employs an XGBoost model trained on demographic and health-related features, developed using the Tidymodels framework.
→ View the application

title: Projects
text:
Customer Churn Prediction App
An interactive churn prediction tool built with R Shiny for the telecommunications sector. Uses the XGBoost algorithm integrated with the Tidymodels framework to analyze customer attributes and behaviors, providing insights into churn probability.
→ View the application

title: Projects
section: Spatial Distribution of Childhood MN Deficiency
text:
Spatial Distribution of Childhood MN Deficiency
An interactive spatial map showing that childhood micronutrient (MN) deficiency in Ethiopia is most prevalent in the Somali, Afar, and Amhara regions, while Gambela, Addis Ababa, and SNNP regions have the lowest prevalence.
→ View the publication

title: Projects
section: COVID-19 Dashboard: The Case of Ethiopia
text:
COVID-19 Dashboard: The Case of Ethiopia
A comprehensive overview of the COVID-19 epidemic in Ethiopia. Built with R and the Quarto framework, this dashboard provides epidemiological trends, case statistics, and visualizations for evidence-based public health response.
→ View the dashboard
